notes: at the house on elm st, just me and Mick today doing the demolition in the kitchen
ripped out all the old cabinets, what a mess. found some asbestos looking stuff behind the splashback
actually I dont think its asbestos, Mick reckons its just old fibro sheeting. but were gonna leave it and get it tested just to be safe. not touching it
called the boss, he's sending a sample to the lab. should know by thursday
we've cleared out most of the kitchen demo. took like 6 skip bins worth of rubble lol. well 2 skip bins but felt like 6
the floor is in rough shape, the old tiles came up but the adhesive is stuck everywhere. gonna need to grind it
hired a floor grinder from Kennards, picking it up tomorrow morning
client wants to keep the original window frame in the kitchen. its hardwood, pretty good condition actually. we'll work around it
found some old knob and tube wiring behind the wall we opened up in the dining room. that all needs to go obviously. sparky is coming wednesday
took a bunch of photos of the wiring situation so the electrician can see what hes dealing with
Mick cut his hand on some sheet metal, not bad but enough to need a bandaid. got the first aid kit from the ute
logged it in the site diary. minor cut, first aid applied, Mick's fine and kept working
weather today is perfect, sunny about 22 degrees. got the windows open airing the place out cos theres heaps of dust
the neighbor came over asking about noise. told her we'll be done with the loud demo stuff by end of week. she was cool about it
wrapped up at about 3. demo is basically done except for the fibro area. waiting on asbestos test results before we touch that wall
tomorrow we'll start framing the new wall layout in the kitchen. Mick's bringing the nail gun
oh also the plumber needs to come look at moving the sink location. the client changed the kitchen layout AGAIN. third time now. whatever
